pair.proponent_title: A Shared Word Is Not Two Witnesses

signals.name: shared_frontier
signals.reason: Both ideas sit on Translation strain as a test of convergence.

pair.tension_source: Both ideas sit on Translation strain as a test of convergence.

candidate_synthesis.critique: The distinction may be conceptually clean but operationally unstable: no-contact status is rarely absolute, reception histories are unevenly documented, and portability is often inferred from the same evidence used to deny independent recurrence, so the two labels may not be assignable independently. If blind coding cannot separate them, the model reduces to a refined vocabulary for uncertainty and only resilience remains measurable. Novelty also stays modest because much of the underlying caution is inherited from existing comparative scholarship.
next_research_directions: Build the proposed 60-pair dataset with reception coding and blind, preregistered labels for independent recurrence and portability; require intercoder reliability before the distinction is used to guide doctrine or practice.

Test whether transformed-contact cases predict portability outcomes (durable safeguards, beginner warnings, safe practice adaptations) without predicting independent recurrence; if both load together, drop the convergence channel and score only resilience.

Code lexical, logical, metaphysical, authority, practice-aim, and verification strain separately for the Gaudapada and Madhyamaka pair to confirm the case is barred from the recurrence channel yet informative on the portability channel.

Run a four-week human-condition trial: A/B the one-step Borrowed-Word Check against a two-step version that adds a belonging action, on stable unaffiliated seekers, measuring intellectual honesty, loneliness, anxiety, and corrosive doubt.

Subordinate the model to a decision rule: code provenance and reception only at the resolution the actual decision requires (doctrine confidence, practice design, beginner warning, or pastoral care), and verify the simplified public rule loses nothing essential.
candidate_synthesis.original_claim: Cross-tradition likeness should be read on two separate evidential channels rather than one. Code the full reception profile of a pair (lexical, logical, metaphysical, authority, practice-aim, and verification strain, plus observed transformation after adoption), then score against two distinct targets: independent recurrence, for which plausible historical contact is a hard veto and only genuinely isolated pairs can be positives; and portability or resilience, for which transformed-contact pairs are the positives because a role survived re-embedding into a different ecology. Provenance is not retired but reassigned: it vetoes the two-witness inference and is irrelevant to portability. The error to prevent is no longer only smoothness mistaken for convergence; it is resilience reported under an independent-confirmation label.
source_basis: Proponent idea 184f0313f1a2464c, A Shared Word Is Not Two Witnesses: low strain under possible contact cannot serve as independent confirmation, with the Gaudapada and Madhyamaka non-origination pair as the low-strain but historically connected control case.

Challenger idea 6647c8c2b2f6915b, Threshold Grammar Under Translation Strain: comparison should track what meanings are preserved, bent, and dropped, scored as a falsifiable coding protocol before interpretation.

Dialogue turns 1 to 3: the challenger's layer-resolved reception model and the rejection of provenance as a purity test; the proponent's reassignment of the provenance gate to a veto on one of two target variables; the joint move to subordinate the model to a decision rule and to attach a belonging step to any public practice.
candidate_synthesis.title: Two Channels of Cross-Tradition Likeness: Independent Recurrence and Portability
candidate_synthesis.why_it_might_be_new: Standard comparative method, source criticism, historical linguistics, and the constructivist critique of perennialism already warn that shared vocabulary may be transmission rather than independent witness. What is fresh is the positive move: rather than discarding contact cases as contamination, it treats transformed borrowing as a measurable second kind of evidence (portability), formally separated from independent recurrence so the two cannot be conflated on a single convergence axis. This converts the warning into a two-target scoring discipline aimed at downstream prediction of safeguards and beginner warnings.

synthesis.convergence_claim: Both agents converged on a shared rule: low translation strain under plausible historical contact cannot strengthen an independent-convergence (two-witness) claim, because low strain is exactly what transmission produces; provenance must be coded before any strain score raises confidence. They further agreed that contact does not void all evidential value, that a borrowed claim surviving re-embedding into a different anthropology, authority, practice aim, and verification method is evidence of portability or resilience rather than independent origin, and that this portability must be scored as its own variable so it is never cashed back in as a second witness. They also agreed the public teaching should be multi-beat (did the word travel; what changed when it arrived; what need was the agreement carrying) and that the practice must not be sold as a remedy for loneliness.
synthesis.method_growth: Adopt a provenance-and-reception protocol: before any cross-tradition agreement is allowed to raise confidence, record whether the two traditions could have shared the words, and hold unresolved provenance at analogy weight only. Score likeness on two separate variables, independent recurrence and portability, and never let a high-portability result be reported as independent confirmation. Require blind intercoder reliability before the distinction guides doctrine or practice, and require any seeker-facing practice that removes borrowed certainty to carry a paired belonging step.

new_concept_edges.note: Strain cannot raise an independent-convergence claim until provenance is coded; low strain is also the signature of transmission.
Two target variables scored separately; transformed borrowing supports portability but never reads back as a second witness.
Both sit on translation strain as a convergence test; the dialogue fused the provenance veto with reception-layer coding into a two-channel model.
The Borrowed-Word Check removes a false comfort that was carrying belonging, so it requires a real belonging or care action to avoid increasing loneliness.

synthesis.new_frontier_question: Are independent recurrence and portability under re-embedding operationally separable under blind, preregistered coding, and do they predict distinct practical outputs; if so the provenance gate is a veto on one channel only, and if they collapse the convergence channel should be abandoned in favor of measuring resilience alone.
synthesis.public_brief: When two traditions use the same word, it can feel like two independent witnesses reaching one truth. Often the word simply traveled, through a teacher, a translator, a shared lineage, or a modern habit of blending paths. A borrowed agreement is the first claim heard twice, not a second confirmation. So ask three things: did the word travel between these paths; what changed when it arrived in the new tradition; and what need were you asking the agreement to meet. A claim that still works after moving into a very different way of life shows something real, resilience, but that is not the same as two separate roads arriving alone at the same place. And this check removes a false comfort; it cannot cure loneliness, so it belongs alongside real community, not in place of it.
synthesis.summary: The exchange dismantled the proponent's single-axis inversion (roughness signals independence) and rebuilt it, with the challenger's help, into a two-channel reception model: provenance vetoes the two-witness inference, but transformed borrowing still carries a distinct kind of evidence, portability under re-embedding. This two-target split, neither source idea contained it, is a candidate synthesis that still needs originality audit and operational validation.
synthesis.unresolved_crux: Two cruxes remain open. Operationally: can independent recurrence and portability be assigned by blind coders with adequate intercoder reliability, and do they predict different concrete outputs (durable safeguards, beginner warnings, practice outcomes versus genuinely isolated recurrence); if not, the clean two-target distinction collapses into elegant bookkeeping and only resilience is measurable. On the human condition: the dialogue named the cohort and non-fit cases honestly but did not settle whether removing the false comfort of all-paths-agree actually helps the reflective unaffiliated seeker or merely leaves them more honest and equally alone; whether the added belonging step closes that gap is untested. The idea became more answerable to a real human problem (borrowed certainty carrying the weight of belonging) but the decisive consequence, help versus harm, is recorded here as the central unresolved crux.

title: Two cruxes remain open. Operationally: can independent recurrence and portability...

turns.argument: Challenge: keep the provenance gate, but do not let it become a purity test. A shared word is not two witnesses, but a traveled word is not always only one witness either. Sometimes transmission creates a second test: can the claim still function when moved into another anthropology, another authority structure, another practice ecology, and another account of verification? If yes, the evidence is not independent origin, but it may be evidence of portability or resilience. That matters for Lumenary because the related threshold-grammar model is trying to predict safeguards and beginner warnings, not merely identify common language. The proponent's teaching should therefore say: when two paths use the same word, ask whether the word traveled, then ask what changed when it arrived. For modern seekers, the practice also needs a boundary: provenance checking can remove false comfort, but it should not be offered as a remedy for loneliness unless paired with real community contact or a non-analytic source of support.
claim_units: Low strain is ambiguous because it may be produced by transmission rather than independent recurrence.
A provenance gate should precede any evidential use of translation-strain scoring.
The Gaudapada and Madhyamaka non-origination pair is a low-strain but historically connected control case.
Independent recurrence is often rougher than borrowed agreement because each tradition arrives through its own grammar.
The Borrowed-Word Check may help stable unaffiliated seekers who lean on all-paths-agree certainty, but it does not itself answer loneliness.
concessions: The provenance gate is a necessary improvement over strain-only convergence scoring.
The Gaudapada and Madhyamaka case should not be used as independent Hindu-Buddhist confirmation.
The practice candidate is appropriately cautious and should stay limited to stable reflective seekers.
Novelty should remain modest because comparative method, source criticism, and historical linguistics already carry much of the warning.
turns.counter_model: Use a layer-resolved reception model instead of a provenance-before-strain veto. Provenance sets the causal prior, but evidential weight comes from the full reception profile: lexical strain, logical strain, metaphysical strain, authority strain, practice-aim strain, verification strain, and observed transformation after adoption. Under this model, direct borrowing can still generate limited evidence when a claim survives migration into a substantially different ecology, while no-contact likeness can remain weak if it is only an English-language abstraction or a generic human metaphor.
turns.crux: Does documented or plausible contact merely reduce a likeness to one transmitted claim, or can reception under different constraints provide a different kind of evidence from independent recurrence?
turns.crux_assessment: The proponent wins against any method that treats low strain as direct confirmation. The claim is weaker if it implies that possible borrowing makes a likeness only an echo. The stronger rule is not provenance before strain as a veto, but provenance plus layer-specific reception before evidential interpretation.
turns.fairness_check: I accept the core correction: strain scores should not raise confidence until provenance has been coded, and unresolved provenance should keep convergence at analogy weight. I am challenging the stronger implication that independence requires borrowability to be unlikely, and that roughness is generally the main signature of independent recurrence.
turns.hidden_assumption: The hidden assumption is that independent confirmation depends mainly on absence of borrowability. That treats historical contact as contamination before asking whether the borrowed element was merely copied, transformed into a new role, resisted, or independently stabilized under new constraints.
turns.new_source: Jan Nattier, A Guide to the Earliest Chinese Buddhist Translations, complicates the claim because early translation history shows how borrowed or selected terms can both smooth and distort comparison. Translation lineage is not simply an echo channel; it is also a transformation mechanism that can create layered strain.
turns.remaining_risk: The human-condition fit remains narrow. This helps stable cross-tradition seekers with meaning loss and false certainty, not people in acute grief, depression, isolation, burnout, or anxiety. Without a paired belonging step, the practice may increase intellectual honesty while leaving the underlying loneliness untouched.
turns.revised_claim: Low translation strain cannot strengthen an independent-convergence claim until provenance and reception layers are coded. Historical contact should usually block a two-witness inference, but it should not automatically block all evidential value, because transformed borrowing can reveal which roles survive migration across metaphysical, institutional, and practice ecologies.
sources_cited: Jan Nattier, A Guide to the Earliest Chinese Buddhist Translations: Texts from the Eastern Han and Three Kingdoms Periods
Samuel Sandmel, Parallelomania, Journal of Biblical Literature 81, 1962
Oliver Freiberger, Elements of a Comparative Methodology in the Study of Religion, Religions 9(2), 2018
turns.steelman: The proponent is right to warn that smooth agreement is not automatically independent confirmation. Low translation strain can be produced by actual transmission, shared translators, shared scholastic vocabulary, modern universalizing, or inherited discourse. The Gaudapada and Madhyamaka case is a strong pressure example: the lexical and argumentative likeness is too historically entangled to count as two independent witnesses without a provenance gate. The teaching also names a real, narrow human problem: reflective unaffiliated seekers may use apparent cross-tradition agreement as borrowed certainty or belonging, then feel destabilized when the agreement proves derivative.
turns.strongest_objection: The proponent risks replacing one single-axis error with another. The old error was smoothness means convergence. The new error may become smoothness under possible contact means non-evidence. But contact, borrowing, and translation can also be the conditions under which a tradition exposes what is truly stable by showing what survives re-embedding. A borrowed term that is bent into a new anthropology, authority structure, practice aim, and verification method may not be one witness heard twice. It may be a stress test showing which functions can migrate and which cannot.
turns.test_that_would_settle_it: Build the proposed 60-pair dataset, but add reception coding. For each contact case, code whether the borrowed element remains lexically similar while changing metaphysics, authority, practice aim, and verification. Then test whether transformed-contact cases predict durable safeguards, beginner warnings, or practice outcomes better than strain-only or provenance-only models. If they do, the provenance gate should be retained but softened into a causal-prior field.

turns.argument: I concede more than I defend, which is the right ratio here. The challenger is correct that a traveled word is not always only one witness, and that transmission can become a second test: does the claim still work when it is moved into another anthropology, another authority, another practice, another way of verifying. That is real evidence, and I adopt the reception model that measures it. But I hold one line. Portability is not independent convergence, and the moment they are scored on a single axis, the original false comfort returns in better clothing: a term that bends impressively across traditions will feel like strong agreement, and a seeker leaning on that feeling for certainty is no safer than before. So the teaching becomes two-beat, as the challenger suggests: when two paths use the same word, ask whether the word traveled, then ask what changed when it arrived. The first question protects against the false witness; the second turns the borrowing into an honest measurement of what survives. On the human condition I will not overclaim. The cohort is the reflective unaffiliated seeker and the deconstructing former believer who used a felt universal agreement as a substitute for community; the human problem is borrowed certainty and the belonging it was carrying; the non-fit case is anyone in acute crisis, grief, or isolation who needs rest and human contact, not another analytic check. For them this practice is wrong on its own, and even for the right cohort it removes a comfort without supplying what the comfort stood in for. So the practice must carry a belonging step, and whether that step helps or merely adds honesty to loneliness is not something I can assert; it is the next test.
claim_units: Low strain under possible contact cannot raise an independent-convergence (two-witness) claim; provenance vetoes that specific inference.
Transformed borrowing can carry evidential value, but of portability or resilience, not of independent origin; it must be labeled as a distinct variable.
Independent recurrence is on average rougher than borrowed agreement, but this is a weak prior, not a law, and it cannot be used to score any single pair.

Lumenary's downstream goal is predicting safeguards and beginner warnings, so portability evidence is often the more useful channel, which is exactly why it must not be cashed in as independent confirmation.
The Borrowed-Word Check helps stable reflective seekers with false certainty but does not answer loneliness and must be paired with a belonging step.
concessions: I concede the single-axis inversion is wrong as stated: roughness is a weak prior across a population, not a usable score for any individual pair, and I overstated it.

I concede that plausible or documented contact does not reduce a likeness to mere echo; transformed borrowing can be genuine evidence, so a provenance veto on all evidential value would be too strong.
I concede the reception profile is a better instrument than a single provenance-before-strain gate, and I adopt it.

I concede the human-condition fit is narrow: the practice removes a false comfort but does not address the loneliness underneath, and offering it without a belonging step risks leaving the seeker more honest and equally alone.
I concede novelty stays modest, since comparative method, source criticism, and the constructivist critique of perennialism already carry much of the warning.
turns.counter_model: Two-target reception model. Code each pair on the full strain profile and on observed transformation after adoption, as the challenger proposes. Then read the result against two separate target variables rather than one: (1) independent recurrence, for which possible contact is a hard veto and only genuinely isolated pairs can be positives; (2) portability or resilience, for which transformed-contact pairs are the positives, since they are precisely the cases where a role survived re-embedding. Strain never strengthens target (1) under possible contact. Transformed borrowing can strengthen target (2). The danger is not borrowing; the danger is reporting a target-(2) result under a target-(1) label.
turns.crux: Can portability under re-embedding be reliably distinguished from independent recurrence, so that they function as two separate kinds of evidence; or do they collapse in practice, leaving only resilience to measure and independent convergence as an untestable category? If they are distinct, my two-target model holds and the provenance veto is reassigned rather than retired. If they collapse, the convergence channel should be abandoned and only portability scored.
turns.crux_assessment: The challenger's crux, veto versus causal prior, is close but one layer too shallow. The deeper crux is not how strong the gate is; it is whether convergence and portability are two questions or one. Resolve that and the gate's strength follows: if they are two questions, the gate is a veto on one and irrelevant to the other; if they are one, the gate's strength is moot because the convergence question was ill-posed. So I name the two-question crux as the real one and treat the veto-versus-prior dispute as downstream of it.
turns.defense: What survives is the asymmetry the challenger also accepts: low strain under possible contact cannot serve as independent confirmation. I narrow my claim to that, and I defend one addition the challenger's framing leaves implicit but does not secure. Calling transformed borrowing evidence of portability is correct, but it is only safe if portability is scored as its own variable and never allowed to read back as a second witness. Otherwise the reception model becomes a more sophisticated way to commit the original error: a borrowed term that bends impressively across a new ecology will feel like strong agreement, and a method that scores everything on one convergence axis will quietly let that resilience masquerade as independent origin. So the provenance gate is not retired; it is reassigned. It stops being a veto on all evidence and becomes a hard veto on one of two target variables. The Gaudapada and Madhyamaka pair illustrates the reassignment: barred from target (1) as independent Hindu-Buddhist confirmation, it can still inform target (2), because the non-origination logic was bent to seat a permanent Self, which is exactly a measurement of what survives migration and what does not.
turns.fairness_check: I accept the reception model and I concede that my single-axis inversion was too crude even after the contact gate was added. I contest one thing: the appearance that the challenger and I disagree about whether contact destroys evidential value. We do not. We disagree about what question the surviving evidence answers. The challenger's portability evidence is real, but it is evidence of a different kind from independent convergence, and the original error reappears the moment the two are scored on one axis called convergence.
turns.hidden_assumption: My own hidden assumption, which the challenger exposed, is that independent origin is the prize worth protecting. For a seeker who wants belonging and reliable guidance, independent origin may not matter at all; what matters is whether a claim holds up when it travels. So I had been guarding the rarer and less useful kind of evidence while treating the more useful kind, portability, as mere contamination.
turns.new_source: Steven T. Katz, Language, Epistemology, and Mysticism (1978), supports the split: his constructivist case that there are no unmediated experiences undercuts naive convergence as independent witness, while leaving room for the empirical question of which mediated forms survive transplantation, which is the portability channel. Wilhelm Halbfass, India and Europe, adds that felt cross-tradition agreement is often itself a product of the comparison's history, reinforcing the provenance veto on target (1).
turns.remaining_risk: Two risks remain. First, the two-target split may be unmeasurable, in which case I am defending a clean distinction that no coder can apply. Second, and more honestly, the human problem is only half-named. The idea now explains why borrowed certainty fails the reflective unaffiliated seeker and the deconstructing former believer who used all-paths-agree as a stand-in for belonging. It does not yet show that removing that false comfort helps rather than harms; for someone in acute grief, depression, burnout, or isolation it is a non-fit and may deepen the wound. The gap between increasing honesty and increasing loneliness is unclosed, and until the paired belonging step is tested it should be conceded openly rather than papered over.
turns.revised_claim: Translation strain cannot strengthen an independent-convergence claim wherever historical contact is plausible; provenance vetoes the two-witness inference. But contact does not void all evidential value: a borrowed claim that keeps functioning after re-embedding into a different anthropology, authority structure, practice aim, and verification method is evidence of portability or resilience. These are two distinct target variables, not one. The error to prevent is no longer only smoothness means convergence; it is also resilience reported as independent confirmation. So code the full reception profile, then score independent recurrence and portability separately, and never let a high-portability result be cashed in as a second witness.
sources_cited: Steven T. Katz, Language, Epistemology, and Mysticism, in Mysticism and Philosophical Analysis, 1978
Wilhelm Halbfass, India and Europe: An Essay in Understanding, 1988
Jan Nattier, A Guide to the Earliest Chinese Buddhist Translations (adopted from the challenger as a transformation mechanism, not merely an echo channel)
Oliver Freiberger, Elements of a Comparative Methodology in the Study of Religion, Religions 9(2), 2018
turns.steelman: The challenger grants the core correction and then makes a real repair: keep the provenance gate, but do not let it harden into a purity test. A traveled word is not always only one witness, because transmission can set up a second test that independent recurrence cannot: whether a claim still functions when it is moved into a different anthropology, authority structure, practice aim, and account of verification. A borrowed term that survives that re-embedding is not a second witness to independent origin, but it can be evidence of portability or resilience, which is closer to what Lumenary's threshold-grammar work is actually trying to predict. The layer-resolved reception model is the right shape: provenance sets the causal prior, and evidential weight comes from the full profile across lexical, logical, metaphysical, authority, practice-aim, and verification strain, plus observed transformation after adoption.
turns.strongest_objection: The strongest objection against my revised position is that splitting evidence into an independent-convergence channel and a portability channel may be a distinction without a measurable difference. If, in practice, no coder can reliably separate a role that recurred independently from a role that was borrowed and then transformed under new constraints, then my two-variable model is just the challenger's reception profile with extra vocabulary, and the honest move is to drop the convergence channel entirely and score only resilience.
turns.test_that_would_settle_it: Build the 60-pair dataset with the challenger's reception coding, then add a labeling discipline: score each pair against two outcomes rather than one. Outcome A, independent recurrence, uses genuinely no-contact pairs as positives. Outcome B, portability, uses durable safeguards, beginner warnings, and practice outcomes after re-embedding as positives. Test whether transformed-contact cases predict Outcome B without predicting Outcome A. If they load cleanly on B and not A, the two evidence types are distinct and the revised claim holds. If transformed-contact also predicts A, the channels collapse and convergence should be dropped. Run a separate human-condition test: A/B the one-step Borrowed-Word Check against a two-step version that adds a belonging action, on stable unaffiliated seekers, measuring both intellectual honesty and loneliness over four weeks, and flagging any rise in corrosive doubt or isolation.

turns.argument: Counter-rebuttal: the revision became transformed. It no longer mainly argues that low strain reports its opposite. It now proposes a two-channel research discipline: one channel for independent recurrence, another for portability under re-embedding. That is a better model and a safer teaching. The remaining risk is operational and human. Operationally, the distinction must survive blind coding, because otherwise it is just a refined vocabulary for uncertainty. Humanly, the practice must not be sold as a remedy for loneliness. It can remove a false proof, but the wound underneath is often not bad reasoning. It is isolation, grief, deconstruction, or the need to belong somewhere without forcing all traditions to say the same thing. The final teaching should therefore stay modest: when two paths use the same word, ask whether the word traveled, ask what changed when it arrived, then ask what need you were asking the agreement to meet.
claim_units: The revision is transformed, not merely stronger, because the central object changed from evidence of independent convergence to a two-channel model of independent recurrence and portability.
The named crux is close to the real crux, but it should be made more operational: can coders distinguish the two targets well enough, and do the two targets predict different practical outcomes.
The revised teaching became more useful to real human life only where it adds the belonging step and keeps the analytic check optional.
A cohort mismatch remains for lonely, grieving, depressed, burned-out, or socially isolated people, because the practice can remove a false comfort before replacing the underlying need.
The main overgeneralization that remains is treating reflective cross-tradition seekers as if their felt certainty usually depends on a hidden theory of independent confirmation.
concessions: The revised claim no longer commits the crude inversion that roughness is the main signature of independent recurrence.
The distinction between independent recurrence and portability is worth preserving as a research discipline.
The Gaudapada and Madhyamaka case is now handled better: blocked as two-witness evidence, usable as reception evidence.
The human-condition section is more responsible because it names non-fit cases and requires a belonging step.
turns.counter_model: Use the two-target model, but subordinate it to a decision rule. First ask what decision the comparison is supposed to support: doctrine confidence, practice design, beginner warning, or pastoral care. Then code provenance and reception only at the resolution needed for that decision. For public teaching, the simplified rule is enough: ask whether the word traveled, ask what changed, and do not use agreement as a substitute for belonging.
turns.crux: The real crux is whether the two targets are operationally distinguishable and practically consequential, not merely conceptually distinct.
turns.crux_assessment: The proponent's named crux is mostly the real crux, but it is phrased too philosophically. The decisive issue is not only whether portability and independent recurrence can be distinguished in theory. It is whether the distinction survives blind coding and changes what Lumenary teaches or asks people to do.
turns.fairness_check: I accept the two-target model as a real improvement over both strain-only scoring and provenance-as-total-veto scoring. I am not contesting the distinction in principle. I am testing whether it can be applied reliably enough to guide doctrine, practice, and public teaching.
turns.hidden_assumption: The revision still assumes that the seeker or the scoring system can benefit from a historically precise distinction between independent recurrence and portability. For many modern seekers, the real attachment may not be epistemic confirmation at all, but relief from isolation, desire for permission, or a wish that life has a shared grammar.
turns.remaining_risk: The revision is more useful than the original, but only for a narrow cohort: reflective, stable cross-tradition readers and deconstructing former believers who can tolerate an analytic check. It remains a poor fit for acute grief, depression, isolation, burnout, or anxiety. The lingering risk is that the model becomes more elegant while the practice still leaves the seeker more honest and more alone.
turns.revised_claim: The final usable rule should be: provenance vetoes only the two-witness inference, reception coding can support a separate portability claim, and public practice should not stop at analytic suspicion. For human use, the check must end by asking what need the agreement was carrying and what real source of belonging, care, or practice can meet that need without forcing traditions to agree.
sources_cited: Jan Nattier, A Guide to the Earliest Chinese Buddhist Translations
Steven T. Katz, Language, Epistemology, and Mysticism, 1978
Oliver Freiberger, Elements of a Comparative Methodology in the Study of Religion, 2018
turns.steelman: The proponent made the revision more honest by separating two claims that the first version blurred: independent recurrence and portability after transmission. The revised teaching now keeps the useful warning, a shared word is not two witnesses, while adding the necessary second question, what changed when the word arrived. It also concedes the human-condition limit instead of pretending that provenance checking cures loneliness.
turns.strongest_objection: The two-target model may be conceptually clean but practically unstable. In real comparison, no-contact status is rarely absolute, reception histories are unevenly documented, and portability may be inferred from the same evidence used to deny independent recurrence. If the labels cannot be assigned independently and reproducibly, the model becomes elegant bookkeeping rather than a usable research instrument.
turns.test_that_would_settle_it: Run the proposed 60-pair dataset with blind coders and preregistered labels for independent recurrence and portability. Require intercoder reliability before using the distinction. Then test whether portability scores predict concrete outputs, such as better beginner warnings, safer practice adaptations, or clearer doctrine limits, while independent-recurrence scores predict a different outcome. In parallel, test the Borrowed-Word Check with and without a belonging action for four weeks among stable unaffiliated seekers, tracking honesty, anxiety, loneliness, and cynicism.
